content.body: This document exists to define the governing UX requirement item shape. Use it when describing what users see, can do, cannot do, what happens next, recovery behavior, constraints, non-goals, and proof expectations.

content.title: Purpose
body: A `uxRequirement` answers: what must the product experience do?
It should not answer: which controller owns it, which store computes it, which state variable drives it, which file implements it, or which code path proves it.

content.title: Optional Facets
body: Use optional facets to add precision only where the requirement needs it:

content.title: User-Facing Language
body: Write requirements in product and UX terms:
- "The user can apply the draft and return to updated results."
- "The action stays visible but disabled when the input is invalid."
- "The modal closes after a successful apply."

content.title: Grouped Assertions
body: A single requirement may group related assertions when that keeps a coherent behavior together. For example, one `Apply` requirement may include visibility, disabled behavior, result, and must-not assertions.
Use `testGate.assertions` for the nested proof statements so tests can still map to specific checks.

content.title: Copy Governance
content.body: Copy is not governing just because it appears in a requirement. Mark exact copy with `contentCopy.governing: true` only when the exact wording materially affects behavior, comprehension, legal meaning, or test expectations.
